review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: mcp-server package reaches into top-level shared/ and server/ via sys.path.insert at import time

evidence: channel.py lines 77-89 inject the talky project root onto sys.path so subsequent 'from shared.*' and 'from server.*' imports resolve.
services_factory.py line 16 inserts <root>/shared, line 84 inserts <root>/server before importing 'config.profile_manager' (which does not exist — the module lives at shared/profile_manager.py).
talky_cli.py lines 24-25 and 532 mutate sys.path before importing daemon code.
detail.suggestion: Promote shared/ to a proper workspace member (e.g. talky-shared package) listed in pyproject.toml's tool.uv.workspace.members and depended on by both server/ and mcp-server/. Then delete every sys.path.insert in production code and switch to plain 'from talky_shared.profile_manager import ...'. This removes the boundary fiction and makes dependency direction declarative.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: mcp-server/services_factory.py duplicates shared/service_factory.py with a sys.path shim and a dead import path

evidence: services_factory.py exposes create_stt_service / create_tts_service that simply forward to create_stt_service_from_config / create_tts_service_from_config in shared/service_factory.py.

Line 85 imports 'from config.profile_manager import get_profile_manager' — there is no config/profile_manager.py in server/config/; the real module is shared/profile_manager.py. This code path is broken.
Two services_factory modules exist (shared one and mcp-server one) with overlapping names — exactly the cross-package duplication the rubric flags as a missing shared boundary.
detail.suggestion: Delete mcp-server/src/pipecat_mcp_server/services_factory.py entirely and have mcp-server callers import shared/service_factory.py directly once shared/ is a real package. If a thin facade is needed for type-narrowed STT/TTS returns, keep one file with no sys.path tricks and remove create_services_from_profile (which references the non-existent config.profile_manager).

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: channel.py defers profile_manager imports 9 times inside methods, signaling hub/cycle pressure

evidence: grep counts 9 'from shared.profile_manager import get_profile_manager' calls inside channel.py methods (lines 215, 475, 555, 584, 635, 701, 759, 779, 1054) and 2 in server.py (lines 80, 123).
shared/profile_manager.py is the most-imported module (19 importers) — a hub.
Deferred-import density across mcp_server pattern is flagged 18 files in scan evidence.
detail.suggestion: Hoist 'from shared.profile_manager import get_profile_manager' to module scope in channel.py and server.py once (after fixing the package boundary above). If a true import cycle exists, break it by extracting the small subset of profile_manager types these call sites need into a leaf module (e.g. profile_types.py with LLMBackend dataclass) and import that. Function-level imports should be the exception with a comment, not a 9x pattern.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: voice_daemon (local audio) and talky daemon (mcp-server) are parallel subsystems with no shared lifecycle/boundary

evidence: server/voice_daemon.py is 714 LOC with its own unix-socket protocol; mcp-server runs an unrelated WebRTC/FastMCP daemon on tcp:9090.
shared/daemon_protocol.py defines VOICE_* and LEGACY_* socket/pid paths side-by-side, indicating an incomplete consolidation.
CLAUDE.md acknowledges 'two daemons, 9d02 unification deferred'.
detail.suggestion: Decide and document the boundary: either fold local-audio into the talky daemon as an in-process subsystem behind a stable interface in shared/, or document voice_daemon as an explicit, frozen sidecar with no further code sharing besides daemon_protocol. Then remove LEGACY_* constants in shared/daemon_protocol.py — they are unused-shim noise blurring the boundary.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: services_factory raises ValueError without `from e`, dropping the original traceback at the user-facing setup boundary

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: OpenClaw backend raises inside except without `from err` and has multiple log-only swallow sites that hide backend failures

evidence: existing_issues lists 5x 'Raise inside except without `from err`' and 4x 'Catch block that only logs (swallowed error)' for this file.

Lines 401-403 log a connection error and re-raise (good), but lines 489-491 catch all message-handler exceptions and only log + flip `_connected=False`, leaving the response_queue consumer to time out instead of receiving a typed signal.

Lines 566-568 catch the response wait failure and emit a fallback TextFrame; the underlying error is logged but never propagated to a health/event channel, so callers cannot distinguish 'OpenClaw down' from 'OpenClaw chose silence'.
detail.suggestion: Add `from e` to all re-raises inside except blocks, and signal connection-loss to the response_queue (e.g. enqueue a sentinel exception) so downstream awaiters see a typed failure instead of a silent stall plus a generic timeout.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Bare `except:` in logging_config silently swallows setLevel failures

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: MCP tools inconsistently return `{"error": ...}` dicts vs propagating exceptions

convo_speak (260-274) and convo_listen (277-285) let RuntimeError ('not live') propagate to the MCP framework with no catch.
No comment or contract documents which tools return error envelopes vs which raise — agents/clients must read each tool's body to know.
detail.suggestion: Pick one rule (e.g. 'control-plane tools return error dicts, data-plane tools raise') and document it next to the tool definitions, or apply the same shape uniformly. Either is fine; the split without a stated rule is the problem.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: available_profiles silently returns just the MCP driver if ProfileManager raises

Lines 154-157: `voice_channel = VoiceChannel(...)` constructed eagerly at module scope before the Starlette lifespan even exists.
detail.suggestion: Move FastMCP/VoiceChannel construction and the TTL/grace reads into a `build_app()` factory called from `if __name__ == '__main__'` and the lifespan startup hook. Tools, scripts, or tests that just want to import a tool definition should not pay for ProfileManager bootstrap and VoiceChannel allocation.

review::.::holistic::initialization_coup.summary: Module-level `_http_sessions` dict keeps aiohttp ClientSession refs across event loops

evidence: shared/service_factory.py:14 `_http_sessions: Dict[str, Any] = {}` — populated lazily via `get_http_session()` and never tied to an app lifespan.

shared/service_factory.py:33-49 `close_http_sessions()` falls back to `asyncio.run(...)` when no loop is running. If two event loops run in the same process (e.g. test harness + daemon), sessions bound to one loop will fail when used from the other.

Combined with the syspath hack in services_factory.py:16 (which imports `service_factory` without the `shared.` prefix), the same module can be imported under two names with two separate `_http_sessions` dicts.
detail.suggestion: Move the session cache onto the VoiceChannel / app state object (or a context manager owned by the lifespan), and drop the module-level dict. Take a session as a constructor argument where used. Eliminates loop-binding surprises and the dual-import duplicate-cache failure mode.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::c.summary: cmd_pi and cmd_claude in talky_cli.py are byte-identical apart from the profile string

evidence: talky_cli.py:782-794 cmd_pi defines a local Args class, copies vars(args), constructs Args('pi', ...) and calls cmd_run_client_profile.
talky_cli.py:802-814 cmd_claude does the same with 'claude'.
detail.suggestion: Replace both with a single factory: `def _make_client_profile_cmd(profile_name): def cmd(args): args_dict = {k: v for k, v in vars(args).items() if k != 'func'}; args.profile = profile_name; return cmd_run_client_profile(args); return cmd` and use `pi_parser.set_defaults(func=_make_client_profile_cmd('pi'))`. The local Args class is unnecessary — assigning `args.profile` on the existing Namespace is sufficient since cmd_run_client_profile reads it via getattr.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::p.summary: ProfileManager._load_llm_backends repeats the LLMBackend(...) constructor block in three branches

evidence: shared/profile_manager.py:117-205 the user-override path (149-160), the no-user-extensions path (172-181), the FileNotFoundError fallback (185-193), and the broad-Exception fallback (197-205) each rebuild LLMBackend with the same six-field construction.
_load_voice_backends:207-262 has a parallel triple-branch merge structure for the (backend_type, provider_name) two-level dict.
detail.suggestion: Refactor to a two-stage merge: (1) build a merged dict by `core | defaults_only_for_new_keys | user_extensions` using deep-merge helpers, (2) materialise into `self.llm_backends` with a single LLMBackend(**config) loop. Wrap the user-extension YAML read in a try/except returning {} on FileNotFoundError or unexpected errors so the materialise step has one path. Same shape for voice backends.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::s.summary: server.py defines two near-identical env-var-then-YAML setting readers

evidence: _read_idle_ttl_seconds:66-102 reads TALKY_ROOM_IDLE_TTL_SECS, falls back to settings['room']['idle_ttl_seconds'], parses float-or-infinity, validates positive.
_read_request_leave_grace_seconds:108-146 mirrors the same env→yaml→default ladder with clamp-to-zero instead of infinity handling.
detail.suggestion: Extract a single helper `def _read_room_setting(env_key: str, yaml_key: str, *, default: float | None, allow_infinity: bool, clamp_floor: float | None) -> float | None`. Both call sites collapse to one-line wrappers. This both removes duplication and centralises the env-vs-yaml precedence rule so future settings inherit it.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::b.summary: _build_and_start_pipeline mixes pipeline construction, pong-responder setup, lifecycle handlers, and runner kickoff

evidence: channel.py:1043-1252 in one function: instantiates STT, builds the LLM switcher map (with per-backend try/except), spawns a `_setup_pong_responder` background task that polls for the data channel, builds the transport, constructs VoiceProfileSwitcher and sets the task back on it, builds the turn detector, builds the Pipeline and PipelineTask, registers transport on_client_connected/on_client_disconnected handlers, installs an upstream frame filter and on_user_speaking handler, commits state to self.*, starts the runner task, and registers _on_runner_done.
Each of those is a coherent step but they accumulate to a 200-line body with five distinct concerns.
detail.suggestion: Split into private helpers on VoiceChannel: `_build_llm_switcher(pm) -> (LLMSwitcher, profile_map)`, `_build_voice_pipeline(stt, llm_switcher, voice_switcher, transport, turn_detector) -> (Pipeline, PipelineTask)`, `_attach_lifecycle_handlers(transport, pipeline_task)`, `_attach_pong_responder(connection)`. Keep _build_and_start_pipeline as the orchestrator that calls these in order and commits state. Don't add new types — just method extraction.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::v.summary: voice_daemon._do_listen folds STT setup, pipeline build, two timeout strategies, and result packaging into one function

evidence: server/voice_daemon.py:230-386 builds STT, plays start tone, builds LocalAudioTransport, constructs LLMContextAggregatorPair with VAD + UserTurnStrategies, registers on_user_turn_started and on_user_turn_stopped handlers, runs a silence-monitor coroutine, applies an asyncio.wait_for hard-cap, plays stop tone, and returns one of three result-dict shapes.
result_holder dict pattern signals the function is coordinating async events that would naturally live on a small helper class or be returned via a queue.
detail.suggestion: Extract `_build_listen_pipeline(stt) -> (PipelineTask, result_holder)` and `_run_with_timeouts(task, silence_timeout, listen_timeout, speech_started_flag) -> bool` (returns True if hard-cap tripped). _do_listen becomes: build, play start tone, run-with-timeouts, play stop tone, package result. The two timeouts are independent strategies and reading them as separate helpers makes the contract obvious.

review::.::holistic::contract_coherence:.summary: openclaw.py functions declare `param: str = None` instead of Optional[str]

evidence: load_paired_tokens(openclaw_dir: str = None) at line 40 — default None contradicts annotation
load_device_identity(openclaw_dir: str = None) at line 86 — same pattern

OpenClawLLMService.__init__(*, gateway_url: str = None, agent_id: str = 'main', session_key: str = None, **kwargs) at line 250 — gateway_url and session_key both have None defaults under str annotations
detail.suggestion: Change the annotations to Optional[str] (or `str | None`) on these three signatures so the type matches reality.

review::.::holistic::contract_coherence:.summary: PiLLMService.send_message annotated `-> str` but always raises NotImplementedError

evidence: Line 111: `async def send_message(self, message: str) -> str:` body is exclusively `raise NotImplementedError(...)`
Class is named PiLLMService but does not subclass pipecat's LLMService, so the method exists only to satisfy a duck-type that it cannot actually satisfy.
detail.suggestion: Either remove the method (Pi backend has its own UI, no caller path) or annotate it `-> typing.NoReturn` and document that this backend deliberately does not support direct injection.

review::.::holistic::contract_coherence:.summary: close_http_sessions() returns before sessions actually close when called from a running loop

evidence: Line 33–49: docstring says 'Call this during application shutdown', but the running-loop branch does `loop.create_task(_close_sessions())` and returns immediately, so the caller has no way to know when sessions are actually closed.
The non-loop branch uses asyncio.run and does block — behavior diverges by call context, which the docstring does not warn about.
detail.suggestion: Make it `async def close_http_sessions()` and have callers await it. If a sync shim is needed for non-async shutdown paths, keep it but document that it is best-effort and only blocks when no loop is running.

review::.::holistic::contract_coherence:.summary: Public service factory helpers lack return annotations despite being part of a typed module

evidence: shared/service_factory.py:74 _create_service_from_backend_config(...) — no return annotation
shared/service_factory.py:112 create_stt_service_from_config(provider: str, **kwargs) — no return annotation
shared/service_factory.py:132 create_tts_service_from_config(provider: str, **kwargs) — no return annotation
shared/voice_config.py:16 create_tts_for_profile(voice_profile_name=None, provider=None, voice_id=None) — no parameter or return annotations
shared/voice_config.py:44 create_vad_analyzer() — no return annotation
detail.suggestion: Annotate the factories with their concrete return types (`-> Any` is acceptable for the dynamic-import factories; `-> SileroVADAnalyzer` for create_vad_analyzer; Optional[str] params on create_tts_for_profile).

review::.::holistic::contract_coherence:.summary: switch_profile / switch_voice MCP tools document `-> dict` but bury errors as {'error': str} silently

evidence: Lines 341–355 and 367–383: catch (RuntimeError, ValueError) and return {'error': str(e)}, but the docstring's Returns section says 'Dict with status and active profile name' — error shape is undocumented.
Other tools (start_convo, list_profiles) consistently include 'success' or just raise; this is the outlier.
detail.suggestion: Either let the exception propagate (FastMCP serializes it) for consistency with the other tools, or document the {'error': str} branch explicitly in the Returns section so callers know to check for it.

review::.::holistic::logic_clarity::spur.summary: Multiple `async def` methods in shared/client_launcher.py never await anything and use blocking subprocess/sleep calls

evidence: _launch_pi (line 30) is async but only calls subprocess.run/Popen.
trigger_voice_command (line 141) uses time.sleep + webbrowser.open with no awaits.
stop_all (line 161) iterates self.processes synchronously; stop (line 224) is a one-line log.

DaemonManager.ensure_running (line 187) is async but every call inside is blocking subprocess.run / time.sleep — it is awaited from talky_cli.py:891 and _launch_claude under the impression that it yields, but it does not.
detail.suggestion: Either drop the `async` keyword on these methods (and update the two callers in talky_cli.py and _launch_claude to call them synchronously), or replace the blocking subprocess.run/time.sleep calls with `asyncio.create_subprocess_exec` and `asyncio.sleep` so the coroutine actually cooperates. Pick one — the current shape promises concurrency the implementation cannot deliver.

review::.::holistic::logic_clarity::spur.summary: OpenClawLLMService._clear_pending_responses is async but performs only synchronous Queue.get_nowait/delattr

evidence: Lines 295-305: `async def _clear_pending_responses(self)` drains a Queue with get_nowait inside a while loop — no await, no I/O.
Callers must `await` this to get clearing semantics; making it async hints at suspension points that do not exist.
detail.suggestion: Drop `async` from `_clear_pending_responses` and update callers (or, if the function is expected to grow into something that performs awaits, leave a comment locking in the contract). Today it is a sync helper masquerading as a coroutine.

review::.::holistic::type_safety::talkyp.summary: resolve_talky_profile passes Optional[str] fields into accessors typed as `name: str`

evidence: TalkyProfile.llm_backend and .voice_profile are typed Optional[str] = None (lines 54–55).

resolve_talky_profile (line 442) calls self.get_llm_backend(tp.llm_backend) and self.get_voice_profile(tp.voice_profile) — both accessors are annotated `name: str` (lines 415, 418), so a None value silently violates the signature and only produces ValueError because dict.get(None) returns None.
Loaders set llm_backend / voice_profile via `config.get(...) or self.defaults.get(...)` (lines 323–324, 346–347), which can resolve to None when both are missing.
detail.suggestion: Either tighten the accessor signatures to accept Optional[str] and return Optional[...] (already do), or guard explicitly in resolve_talky_profile: raise ValueError if tp.llm_backend is None / tp.voice_profile is None before dispatch. Cleanest: split TalkyProfile into a raw config dataclass and a resolved one where llm_backend/voice_profile are required str.

review::.::holistic::type_safety::daemon.summary: send_message/recv_message use bare `dict` instead of dict[str, Any]

evidence: Line 22: `def send_message(sock: socket.socket, data: dict) -> None` — `data` is annotated as bare `dict` with no value type.
Line 28: `def recv_message(sock: socket.socket, timeout: float = 30.0) -> dict` — same.
These functions are the wire protocol surface used by both voice_daemon.py and clients; loose typing here propagates Any through every call site.
detail.suggestion: Annotate as `dict[str, Any]` (or define a `Mapping[str, Any]`/TypedDict if the message envelope is stable, e.g. `{'type': str, 'payload': ...}`).

review::.::holistic::type_safety::unused.summary: AsyncGenerator imported in server/backends/base.py but never used

evidence: Line 9: `from typing import Any, AsyncGenerator, Dict, Optional` — AsyncGenerator has no references in the file.
detail.suggestion: Remove AsyncGenerator from the import list.
